description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by vertical struts. The wings and tail surfaces are covered in a reddish-brown material with white roundels near the wingtips, suggesting a historical military or training aircraft. The nose features a single propeller and a visible engine cowling, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a mounted machine gun in front of the pilot's seat. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both with a similar reddish-brown finish. The landing gear is a fixed taildragger configuration, with two main wheels and a small tail skid. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, likely constructed primarily from wood and fabric with some metal components.
description: A vintage wooden biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and fixed taildragger landing gear, finished in reddish-brown with white roundels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and open cockpit behind the engine cowling.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, fixed landing gear, and the open cockpit with visible machine gun.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal tail surfaces, and the tail skid.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the same features as the left, with clear visibility of the wing struts and tail assembly.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or sweep; dihedral angle not apparent.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
